NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR TERMINATING SACTIONS, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF PAYNE & FEARS, LLP HEARING SET FOR AUG-23-2011 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

PLAINTIFF PAYNE & FEARS, LLP MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS TWICE FAILED TO SIT FOR DEPOSITION DESPITE A SUBPOENA AND THE WRITTEN AND ORAL ORDERS OF THIS COURT. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF'S EXCUSE THAT SHE WON'T COME TO CALIFORNIA BECAUSE THERE IS A BENCH WARRANT OUT FOR HER ARREST (WHICH WAS NOT TOLD TO THIS COURT PREVIOUSLY) DOES NOT PROVIDE AN ADEQUATE EXCUSE TO AVOID ANOTHER ORDER OF THIS COURT. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO APPEAR AT PLAINTIFF'S LAW OFFICE ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 26, 2011 AT 9:00 AM FOR DEPOSITION. THE COURT WILL STAY THE SANCTIONS ORDER PENDING DEFENDANT'S COMPLETION HER DEPOSITION. JUDGE LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: LORETTA NAJERA, CSR #5153 (302)

DEFENDANT DOES NOT PRESENT SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF FILED THIS ACTION IN THE WRONG COURT. DEFENDANT'S MOTION BASED ON CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES IS PREMATURE BECAUSE THERE IS NO ANSWER ON FILE. SEE EASTON V. SUPERIOR COURT, (1970) 12 CAL.APP.3D 243, 245. MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: James McBride, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Sonia Wong #11288.

RODNEY B. SORENSEN Esq. appeared for plaintiff(s) was placed under oath and direct examined.

Default has been entered against the Defendant(s) KATHLEEN SMITH

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff(s), and against the Defaulted Defendant(s) KATHLEEN SMITH for Judgment as follows:

Damages/Principal - $ 705,572.20
Prejudgment Interest - $ 108,441.30
Costs - $ 3346.78
Total judgment in the amount of $ 817,360.28

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-04-2011 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sactions
Off Calendar Aug-23-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF PAYNE & FEARS, LLP MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS.
Appearances: Daniel F. Lula of Payne & Fears LLP (949.797.1269) for plaintiff and moving party; Erin Gray of the Rupprecht Law Firm (722.2309) for defendant and responding parties.
Argued and the court modified its ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion for terminating sanctions is granted. Defendant has twice failed to sit for deposition despite a subpoena and the written and oral orders of this court. Moreover, plaintiff's excuse that she won't come to California because there is a bench warrant out for her arrest (which was not told to this court previously) does not provide an adequate excuse to avoid another order of this court. Defendant is ordered to appear at plaintiff's law office on Friday, August 26, 2011 at 9:00 am for deposition. The court will stay the sanctions order pending defendant's completion her deposition. (302/LMG) Judge Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153

APPEARANCES: Daniel F. Lula, Esq. of Payne & Fears, LLP 949/851-1100 for pltf & opposing party (appearing via CourtCall); John Greerty, Esq. 925/768-4905 specially appearing for Michael W. Rupprecht, Esq. of Law Offices of Michael W. Rupprecht, 925/837-0867 atty for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENY MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE. DEFENDANT DOES NOT PRESENT SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF FILED THIS ACTION IN THE WRONG COURT. DEFENDANT'S MOTION BASED ON CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES IS PREMATURE BECAUSE THERE IS NO ANSWER ON FILE. SEE EASTON V. SUPERIOR COURT, (1970) 12 CAL.APP.3D 243, 245. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
